mnemonics: Hadoop: H - Handle large datasets, A - Analyze, D - Data
Spark: S - Speed, P - Process, A - Analytics, R - Reliability, K - Knowledge
keyPoints: Hadoop Word Count Program: Use MapReduce to process text files, split data into key-value pairs, and store results in a file.
Spark Word Count Program: Utilize RDDs (Resilient Distributed Datasets), map, filter, and reduce to process data in parallel, resulting in a DataFrame with word counts.
Key Concept: Use the Hadoop File System (HFS) to store input files and the OutputFormat class to write results to a file.
Use the Spark Context (SC) to create an RDD from a file, apply transformations, and collect the results.
Hadoop Word Count: Write a Map function to split words from lines, a Reduce function to count occurrences, and a Mapper to handle multiple words.
Spark Word Count: Define a map function to split words, a filter function to remove punctuation, and a reduce function to count occurrences.
Example Code: Hadoop Word Count (java) and Spark Word Count (scala) can be used to illustrate the concepts.
Word Count Program: A simple program that calculates word frequencies in a text file, useful for analyzing text data.
